articles.title: Protecting lions and people: the biologist dedicated to tackling human-wildlife conflict
articles.description: Moreangels Mbizah has blazed a trail in Zimbabwe as the first black African woman to found a conservation organisation in the country

articles.title: Media space expands as nation marks World Press Freedom Day
articles.description: Sikhulekelani Moyo/ Raymond Jaravaza ZIMBABWE yesterday joined the rest of the world in commemorating World Press Freedom Day, with the Government highlighting a significant expansion in the country’s media landscape...

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: 14 People Killed, Dozens Injured in Bus Accident Along Harare–Nyamapanda Highway
more.description: 14 People Killed, Dozens Injured in Bus Accident Along Harare–Nyamapanda Highway 14 people died on Tuesday after a long-distance luxury bus they were travelling in was involved in a crash along the Harare–Nyamapanda Highway. The fatal crash occurred on Tuesday afternoon near Suswe, leaving dozens injured and prompting an urgent response from emergency services. Also [...] The post 14 People Killed, Dozens Injured in Bus Accident Along Harare–Nyamapanda Highway appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: Byo residents call for end to water shedding
more.description: Source: Byo residents call for end to water shedding — CITEZW Bulawayo residents say they are hopeful that water supplies will improve after a strong rainy season boosted levels in the city’s dams. The residents expressed optimism that the Bulawayo City Council (BCC) could ease or end the long-running water rationing system, which has been [...] The post Byo residents call for end to water shedding appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ZAPU claims constitutional changes could curb freedoms
more.description: ZAPU has accused the Zanu PF-led government of becoming “aggressive and dangerous” in the wake of the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CA3), warning that the country’s political environment is increasingly repressive and intolerant of dissent.

more.title: After Court Defeat, Mnangagwa Orders Publication Of State Land Inquiry Report
more.description: Source: After Court Defeat, Mnangagwa Orders Publication Of State Land Inquiry Report ⋆ Pindula News President Emmerson Mnangagwa has ordered the publication of the Commission of Inquiry Report into the Sale of State Land, complying with a High Court directive. The order was published on 1 May 2026 through General Notice by Chief Secretary to [...] The post After Court Defeat, Mnangagwa Orders Publication Of State Land Inquiry Report appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Harare’s Water Source At Risk As MPs Allocated Stands On Monavale Wetland
more.description: Source: Harare’s Water Source At Risk As MPs Allocated Stands On Monavale Wetland ⋆ Pindula News Monavale Wetland, or Monavale Vlei, in Harare, is one of only seven Wetlands of International Importance in Zimbabwe. It is now under threat, as 23 Members of Parliament have allegedly been allocated stands on the fragile ecosystem. According to [...] The post Harare’s Water Source At Risk As MPs Allocated Stands On Monavale Wetland appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Food is back — but can Zimbabweans afford it?
more.description: Source: Food is back — but can Zimbabweans afford it? -Newsday Zimbabwe Famine Early Warning Systems Network ZIMBABWE’S 2026 harvest is improving food availability after a difficult lean season, but rising prices, low incomes and below-average yields in some areas mean many households may still struggle to afford food, according to a new report by [...] The post Food is back — but can Zimbabweans afford it? appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ZiG’s Future Depends On Purchasing Power, Not Paper Quality, Says Economist
more.description: Economist Luxon Zembe has said that long-term confidence in the ZiG will depend on the currency’s ability to retain value, rather than on the recent launch of better-quality banknotes.

more.title: US$70 million down the drain as Mangudya gives brutal assesment of COTTCO’s mismanagement
more.description: MUTAPA Investment Fund (MIF) Chief Executive Officer (CEO) John Mangudya has offered a brutal indictment of the mismanagement at the Cotton Company of Zimbabwe (COTTCO), which is financially bleeding despite the government sinking in excess of US$70 million. COTTCO was recently placed under a corporate rescue practitioner by the board in a bid to pluck [...] The post US$70 million down the drain as Mangudya gives brutal assesment of COTTCO’s mismanagement appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: A dangerous overreach – Constitutional Movement slams AG Mabhiza’s warning against cabinet dissent
more.description: The Zimbabwe Constitutional Movement (ZICOMO) has criticised Attorney General (AG) Virginia Mabhiza over her warning that ministers and vice presidents must not publicly oppose Cabinet decisions, calling the stance a “dangerous overreach” that undermines constitutional rights. In a statement on Tuesday, ZICOMO said it noted “with grave concern” the AG’s remarks that government officials “cannot [...] The post A dangerous overreach – Constitutional Movement slams AG Mabhiza’s warning against cabinet dissent appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Women expose Zimbabwe’s justice system failures; decry legal gaps, police inaction
more.description: WOMEN from across Zimbabwe have raised serious concerns over gaps in the justice system, saying weak laws, lack of resources and discrimination are denying them justice. The concerns emerged during an indaba on access to justice for women and girls organised by Women and Law in Southern Africa (WLSA). The meeting brought together officials from the [...] The post Women expose Zimbabwe’s justice system failures; decry legal gaps, police inaction appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: ZITF sees 61% private sector exhibitor turnout
more.description: INDUSTRY and Commerce Minister, Mangaliso Ndlovu has revealed that 61% of exhibitors who made it to the 2026 Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF) were from the private sector with the remainder being government entities. ZITF is the country’s premier multi-sectorial exhibition, held annually in Bulawayo. The 66th edition, ZITF 2026, recently concluded on April 25, [...] The post ZITF sees 61% private sector exhibitor turnout appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: ‘We seek justice, not convictions’ – Matanda-Moyo issues tough warning to prosecutors
more.description: Prosecutor-General Loice Matanda-Moyo has issued a strong warning against weak prosecutions, declaring that justice not convictions, must define the work of the National Prosecuting Authority of Zimbabwe (NPAZ). Speaking at the official opening of a Superior Courts Appeals procedures training in Harare, Matanda-Moyo said prosecutors must meet a higher constitutional standard and abandon practices that [...] The post ‘We seek justice, not convictions’ – Matanda-Moyo issues tough warning to prosecutors appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Ramaphosa told Mnangagwa to stop Constitutional Amendment Bill, drop thoughts of firing Chiwenga – claims former ally
more.description: SOUTH African President Cyril Ramaphosa came to caution President Emmerson Mnangagwa against firing his deputy Constantino Chiwenga and continuing with the highly contentious Constitutional Amendment Bill No.3, former ally Rutendo Matinyarare has claimed. The post Ramaphosa told Mnangagwa to stop Constitutional Amendment Bill, drop thoughts of firing Chiwenga – claims former ally appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .
